fact: November 16 is exactly 50 days before January 5th - that's like counting down 7 whole weeks plus one extra day!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when squirrels go absolutely nuts burying their winter stash - they can bury up to 25 nuts per hour!
The sun sets before 5 PM in most of Canada and northern US states around this date, making dinner time feel like midnight!
November 16 falls smack in the middle of Scorpio season - the zodiac sign represented by a creature that glows under black light!
This is peak migration time for monarch butterflies in Mexico, where millions create orange clouds so thick they can be seen from space!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, November 16 is when jacaranda trees explode with purple flowers that carpet entire streets!
The moon around this time of year appears extra huge and orange when it rises - it's called the 'Beaver Moon' because beavers are frantically building dams!
November 16 is roughly when the first hard frost hits many northern regions, turning spider webs into glittering crystal art overnight!
This date sits exactly 35 days after the autumn equinox, which means daylight is shrinking by about 2 minutes every single day in northern places!
In northern forests, this is when deer start growing their thick winter coats - they can add up to 30% more fur in just a few weeks!
The Leonid meteor shower peaks around this time, shooting up to 15 'falling stars' per hour across the night sky!
November 16 marks the time when maple trees have dropped most of their leaves, but oak trees stubbornly hang onto theirs until spring - nature's rebels!
